Lucius Valerius Privatus and Lucius Valerius Martialis, freedmen and heirs, took care to have this made. He was not my husband, and I left no children, slaves, or masters. May you fare well. Valerius Secundus, freedman; Valerius Cerdo, freedman; Valerius Crescens, freedman; Valerius Verecundus, freedman; Valerius Abrio, freedman; Valeria Aphrodisia, freedwoman; Valerius Ampliatus, heir and freedman.
EDCS-26600712.summary: This epitaph, erected by freedmen and heirs including Lucius Valerius Privatus and Lucius Valerius Martialis, commemorates Lucius Valerius Seranus, a centurion of Legio IIII Flavia Felix, with a complex familial and legal statement and a list of associated freedmen.

EDCS-26600713.text_edition: \n LAICIM IAL AT IV ICON decurio quaestor municipii Aeli Viminacii ob honorem decurionatus donum dedit loco dato decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-26600713.translation: Laicius Ialatus Iucon, a decurion and quaestor of the municipality of Aelia Viminacium, gave this gift in honor of his decurionate, on land provided by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-26600713.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a local official in Aelia Viminacium, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600715.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius C<hr=RH>estos Tito Flavio Iuliano filio anni I monumentum posuit \n
EDCS-26600715.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Chestos erected this monument for his son, Titus Flavius Iulianus, aged one year.
EDCS-26600715.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Titus Flavius Chestos for his infant son, Titus Flavius Iulianus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600716.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Iulius Seneca vixit annos XL Ulpia Nigrina \n
EDCS-26600716.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Iulius Seneca lived for 40 years. (Erected by) Ulpia Nigrina.
EDCS-26600716.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Quintus Iulius Seneca and likely indicates Ulpia Nigrina as the dedicator, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600717.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Proculus, son of Cipeius.
EDCS-26600717.summary: This is a short funerary inscription recording the name of the deceased, Proculus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27400513.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Epistratus Augustalis coloniae Ratiariae ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus a splendidissimo ordine\n
EDCS-27400513.translation: Marcus Ulpius Epistratus, Augustalis of the colony of Ratiaria, honored with decurional ornaments by the most distinguished council.
EDCS-27400513.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Ulpius Epistratus for receiving distinguished civic honors in the Roman colony of Ratiaria, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27400515.text_edition: \n Deae Orciae sacrum pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Diocletiani et Maximiani Auggustorum ordo amplissimus splendidissimae coloniae Singiduni per Marcos Aurelios Cutiam et Sosonem IIviros curante Simplicio viro egregio dominis nostris Deiocletiano sic III et Maximiano Augustis consulibus\n
EDCS-27400515.translation: Sacred to the goddess Orcia. For the safety of our lords Diocletian and Maximian Augusti, the most honorable council of the most splendid colony of Singidunum, through the duoviri Marcos Aurelios Cutia and Sosonus, with Simplicus, an outstanding man, overseeing, during the consulship of our lords Diocletian (as consul III) and Maximian Augusti.
EDCS-27400515.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the city council of Singidunum to the goddess Orcia for the well-being of Emperors Diocletian and Maximian, dating to their joint consulship, likely between 293 and 305 CE.

EDCS-27400516.text_edition: \n Volcano et et Valerius Varius IIviri et Aureli la et Maximinus aediles et Aureli Mucianus et Ingenianus quaestores Quieto et Veldemiano sic consulibus\n
EDCS-27400516.translation: To Vulcan. Valerius Varius and [---] son of Valerius, duoviri, and Aurelius [---] and Maximinus, aediles, and Aurelius Mucianus and Ingenianus, quaestors, during the consulship of Quiet[us] and Veldemian[us].
EDCS-27400516.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Vulcan by municipal officials (duoviri, aediles, quaestors) of a colony, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-27400517.text_edition: \n M - Iovi votum Aelius Marcus Apamenus donum dedit\n
EDCS-27400517.translation: To Jupiter. Aelius Marcus Apamenus gave this offering in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-27400517.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter, dedicated by Aelius Marcus Apamenus, likely from a Roman provincial context.

EDCS-27400518.text_edition: \n Lucio Calpurnio Tertio veterano ex aquilifero legionis IIII Flaviae felicis vixit nnos XXX Lucius Calpurnius INAMORIO TIVS CONCITATVS armatura legionis eiusdem\n
EDCS-27400518.translation: To Lucius Calpurnius Tertius, a veteran and former aquilifer of the IV Flavia Felix legion, who lived 30 years. Lucius Calpurnius [---]atus, a former member of the same legion's armatura.
EDCS-27400518.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Lucius Calpurnius Tertius, a veteran aquilifer, and also mentions another former soldier of the same legion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27400520.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpiae Piae domo Vindobona vixit annos XXI Aurelius Maximus veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae coniugi pientissimae et Ulpiae Gaiae socrae et matri eius et sibi vivus\n
EDCS-27400520.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Ulpia Pia, from Vindobona, who lived 21 years. Aurelius Maximus, a veteran of the IV Flavia Felix legion, erected this for his most devoted wife, and for Ulpia Gaia, her mother, and for himself, while still alive.
EDCS-27400520.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Maximus, a veteran, for his wife Ulpia Pia, her mother Ulpia Gaia, and himself, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27400521.text_edition: \n D O Sempr<o=E>nio Cobae principi vixit nnos VI Claudiae Bubae et Dotus <f=P>ilis CAE Daizinis uxori eius et filiis eorum pater Valens beneficiarius praefecti EV IA Singiduniensi \n
EDCS-27400521.translation: To Sempronius Cobae, princeps, lived 6 years. To Claudia Buba and her children, and to her husband Dotus, son of Pilius, and their children. Valens, beneficiarius of the prefect of Singidunum, [dedicated this] for his wife, IA, and his children.
EDCS-27400521.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sempronius Cobae, Claudia Buba, her husband Dotus, and their children, set up by Valens, a beneficiarius of the prefect of Singidunum.

EDCS-27400529.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius N Severus vixit \n
EDCS-27400529.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius N. Severus lived.
EDCS-27400529.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Publius N. Severus.

EDCS-27400532.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavia C<hr=RH>este vixit nnos XLII hic sita est Velox unicus maritus Iulianus Valentina Velocianus filii matri pientissimae bene merenti faciendum curaverunt \n
EDCS-27400532.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia Chreste lived 42 years. Velox, her only husband, and Iulianus Valentina Velocianus, her sons, arranged for her burial here, to their most devoted and deserving mother.
EDCS-27400532.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Chreste, set up by her husband and sons, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-27800783.text_edition: \n Aurelius Lucianus miles legionis IIII Flaviae vixit annis XXXV Cemeia mater et Aelia ca coniux carissima et fratres posuerunt bene merenti Dis Manibus\n
EDCS-27800783.translation: Aurelius Lucianus, soldier of the IIII Flaviae legion, lived 35 years. His mother Cemeia, his dearest wife Aelia Ca, and his brothers set this up for the deserving one. To the departed spirits.
EDCS-27800783.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Lucianus, a soldier of the IIII Flaviae legion, set up by his mother, wife, and brothers.

EDCS-27800785.text_edition: \n decurio municipii frater et heres bene merenti\n
EDCS-27800785.translation: To the deserving one, from his brother and heir, a decurio of the municipium.
EDCS-27800785.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely honoring a decurio of a municipium, set up by his brother and heir.

EDCS-27800786.text_edition: \n Nonius SaIgnus qui et signo Dalmatius beneficiarius consularis legionis IIII Flaviae vixit annos XXXX menses III dies XVIII stipendiorum XXIII Iulia Heraclia coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27800786.translation: Nonius Salgnus, also known as Dalmatius, beneficiarius of the consular legate of the IIII Flaviae legion, lived 40 years, 3 months, and 18 days, with 23 years of service. Iulia Heraclia set this up for her deserving wife.
EDCS-27800786.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nonius Salgnus Dalmatius, a beneficiarius of a consular legate, set up by his wife Iulia Heraclia.

EDCS-27800788.text_edition: \n Alma lavacrorum de saxis decido lympha et sunt ex lapide perfectae balneae pulchrae laetis inque locis natus lacus id tamen ipsis tunc cum sospes erat coniux sacravit in usum emeritis quondam Alexandreo nomine dignae raucisoni lapidoso cadunt de fonte liquores tam laudati operis dominus famosus et auctor in suae memoriam voluit consecrare maritae ut tamen et lector nomen cognoscere possis singulae declarant exordia litterae primae Aelia cum Tertia subole de coniuge Fausta\n
EDCS-27800788.translation: The abundant water from the baths, made of stone, flows from a spring. These beautiful baths, situated in pleasant locations, were dedicated by his wife to the one who, when he was alive, was named Alexandreo. The lord and founder of such a praised work wished to consecrate it in memory of his wife, so that the reader might also know her name. The initial letters spell out: Aelia with her third child for her husband, Fausta.
EDCS-27800788.summary: This is a dedication of a bath complex to a man named Alexandreo, by his wife Aelia and their children, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-27800789.text_edition: \n In honorem Imperatoris Caesaris Augusti Caius Memmius Cyriacus Augustalis IIviralibus ornamentis ab ordine et ab ordine in se conlatum statuam argenteam ex pondo libris XL \n
EDCS-27800789.translation: In honor of the Emperor Caesar Augustus, Caius Memmius Cyriacus, holding the rank of Augustalis and having been honored with the IIviral office by the order, dedicated a silver statue weighing 40 pounds.
EDCS-27800789.summary: This is a dedication of a silver statue to Emperor Caesar Augustus by Caius Memmius Cyriacus, an Augustalis and former IIvir, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-27800790.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Quesidio Cai filio Praesenti decurioni et quinquennali primo municipii Publi Aeli Dru et decurioni municipii Aeli Viminacii vixit annos LXIII Lucius Quesidius Praesentianus filius et Lucilius Regulinus IIvir quinquennalis municipii Aelii et Aurelius Florus quinquennalis municipii Aeli Viminacii sribendum curaverunt hic situs est\n
EDCS-27800790.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Quesidius Praesentianus, son of Lucius Quesidius Praesens, decurio and first quinquennalis of the municipium of Publius Aelius Dru, and Lucius Regulinus, IIvir quinquennalis of the municipium of Aelius, and Aurelius Florus, quinquennalis of the municipium of Aelius Viminacius, arranged for this to be inscribed. He is buried here.
EDCS-27800790.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Quesidius Praesens, a decurio and quinquennalis, set up by his son and two quinquennales officials of related municipia.

EDCS-27800791.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Telestae vixit annos XXXX Tittha mater heres bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-27800791.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Telestae lived 40 years. His mother Tittha, his heir, set this up for him as deserved.
EDCS-27800791.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her son who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-27800793.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus templum Terrae Matris conlapsum restituit sub cura Cassi Ligurini procuratoris Augusti instantia sic Publio Fundanio Eutychete et Publio Aelio Muciano colonis\n
EDCS-27800793.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus restored the temple of the Mother Earth which had collapsed, under the supervision of Cassius Ligurinus, procurator of the emperor, at the urging of Publius Fundanius Eutychetes and Publius Aelius Mucianus, colonists.
EDCS-27800793.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to Terra Mater by Emperor Septimius Severus, overseen by a procurator and local colonists, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27800794.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aemilia Sda vixit nnos Tiberius Claudius Ins coniugi e\n
EDCS-27800794.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Sda lived... years. To Tiberius Claudius Ins, her husband.
EDCS-27800794.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aemilia Sda, recording her life span and dedicating it to her husband, Tiberius Claudius Ins.

EDCS-29000035.text_edition: \n Dolicheno Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Surianus ex voto renovavit\n
EDCS-29000035.translation: Aurelius Surianus restored this to Iuppiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, according to his vow.
EDCS-29000035.summary: This dedication records Aurelius Surianus fulfilling a vow by restoring a shrine to Jupiter Dolichenus.

EDCS-29000036.text_edition: \n IL Aurelius Nicomachus beneficiarius consularis legionis VII Claudiae animo libens votum solvit Imperatore Antonino IIII et Balbino II consulibus\n
EDCS-29000036.translation: Aurelius Nicomachus, a *beneficiarius consularis* of the Seventh Claudian Legion, willingly fulfilled his vow during the consulship of Antoninus IIII and Balbinus II.
EDCS-29000036.summary: This inscription records a soldier, Aurelius Nicomachus, fulfilling a vow during the consulship of Antoninus and Balbinus, placing it in the reign of either Caracalla or Elagabalus.

EDCS-29000037.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Pisto militavit annis XXVII beneficiarius vixit annis LXXIII Valeria Faventina coniux vixit annis LXXII Valerius Paulinus tribunus legionis Vincentius Exsuperius et Pistus circitor filii Sisia filia patri bene merenti sepulcrum honoraverunt\n
EDCS-29000037.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Pisto served for 27 years as a *beneficiarius* and lived for 73 years. His wife Valeria Faventina lived for 72 years. His sons Vincentius Exsuperius and Pistus, *circitores*, and his daughter Sisia honored their deserving father with this tomb.
EDCS-29000037.summary: This tomb inscription honors Aurelius Pisto, a *beneficiarius* who served 27 years and lived to 73, by his wife and children, likely dating to the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29000039.text_edition: \n Lucio Anatinio Luci liberto Phoebo Augustali coloniae Ulpiae Ratiariae ornato ornamentis decurionalibus immunitate munerum publicorum concessa ab ordine coloniae eiusdem Augustales coloniae eiusdem aere conlato decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-29000039.translation: To Lucius Anatinus Phoebus, freedman of Lucius, an Augustalis of the colony of Ulpia Ratiaria, adorned with decurional honors and granted exemption from public duties by the town council, the Augustales of the same colony erected this at a cost collected from among themselves by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-29000039.summary: This inscription records the collective honor bestowed by the Augustales of Ulpia Ratiaria upon Lucius Anatinus Phoebus, a prominent member of their order, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29000040.text_edition: \n Augustales coloniae Ulpiae Ratiariae Tito Iulio Paletho ornato decurionalibus ornamantis \n
EDCS-29000040.translation: The Augustales of the colony of Ulpia Ratiaria [erected this] to Titus Julius Palethus, adorned with decurional honors.
EDCS-29000040.summary: This dedication by the Augustales of Ulpia Ratiaria honors Titus Julius Palethus for his decurional honors, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29000041.text_edition: \n elius Sura fratri bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29000041.translation: Aelius Sura erected this for his deserving brother.
EDCS-29000041.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aelius Sura for his brother.

EDCS-29000042.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Macedo |(centurio) legionis VII Claudiae stipendiorum XXVI probatus Paullo et Aproniano consulibus vixit annis L socer eius bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29000042.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Valerius Macedo, a centurion of the Seventh Claudian Legion with 26 years of service, proved [worthy], during the consulship of Paulus and Apronianus. His father-in-law erected this tomb for his deserving [son-in-law], who lived 50 years.
EDCS-29000042.summary: This tombstone commemorates Gaius Valerius Macedo, a centurion of the Legio VII Claudia, who served 26 years and died at 50, erected by his father-in-law during the consulship of Paul and Apronianus (likely in the 3rd century CE).

EDCS-29000043.text_edition: \n XXX Flavius Iucundusr et Aeli Anns et Iucundus s et Gaia heredes Gaianus filii C hic sit- est\n
EDCS-29000043.translation: Flavius Iucundus, Aelius Anns, Iucundus, and Gaia, heirs of the 30 [year-old] Gaianus, son [of...], declare this is his burial place.
EDCS-29000043.summary: This funerary inscription identifies the heirs of Gaianus and marks his burial plot, possibly indicating he died at a young age of 30.

EDCS-29000044.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Cnaeus Clodius Cnaei filius Classicianus tribunus cohortis XIIX voluntariorum civium Romanorum et translatus ab optimis maximisque Imperatoribus in cohortem I Ulpiam Pannoniorum |(miliariam) equitatam votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29000044.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gnaeus Clodius Classicianus, son of Gnaeus, tribune of the 18th cohort of Roman citizen volunteers, and transferred by the best and greatest Emperors to the 1st Ulpian Pannonian Cohort (miliaria) equitata, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-29000044.summary: A military diploma inscription recording a vow paid by a tribune of a volunteer cohort and later transferred to an auxiliary cavalry unit, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-29000045.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Aurelius Iulianus miles libens posuit merito\n
EDCS-29000045.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Paterno Aurelius Iulianus, a soldier, willingly set this up deservedly.
EDCS-29000045.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by a soldier named Paterno Aurelius Iulianus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29000046.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Iulius Se<p=R>tumus sic votum posuit \n
EDCS-29000046.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris, Iulius Septumus set up his vow thus.
EDCS-29000046.summary: A vow dedicated to the woodland deity Silvanus by Iulius Septumus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29000047.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Faustinianus signifer legionis IIII Flaviae vixit nnos XXXII militavit nnos XII Faustina filia vixit nnum I mensem I Aelia Maxima coniugi et filiae bene merentibus posuit sibi ipsa heres\n
EDCS-29000047.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Aurelius Faustinianus, a standard-bearer of the IV Flavia Felix legion, lived 32 years, served 12 years. Faustina, his daughter, lived 1 year and 1 month. Aelia Maxima, his wife, set this up for her well-deserving husband and daughter, and for herself as heir.
EDCS-29000047.summary: A tombstone inscription commemorating Aurelius Faustinianus, a standard-bearer, his wife Aelia Maxima, and their infant daughter Faustina, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29000048.text_edition: \n rior decurio municipii Singiduni vixit nnos XXV et Cais Cassiis Verae vixit annis XIII et Firmio vixit annis IIII filiis eorum Cassia Firma filia heres parentibus bene merentibus posuit\n
EDCS-29000048.translation: A decurion of the municipality of Singidunum lived 25 years, and Cais Cassia Vera lived 13 years, and Firmus lived 4 years; their children. Cassia Firma, their daughter and heir, set this up for her well-deserving parents.
EDCS-29000048.summary: A tombstone inscription for a decurion of Singidunum and his family, erected by their daughter and heir Cassia Firma, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29000049.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Corneliae Cai filiae Priscae quae vixit nnis XVI mensibus VI diebus XVIII Cornelius Priscus RISVIRC\n
EDCS-29000049.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Cornelia Prisca, daughter of Caius, who lived 16 years, 6 months, and 18 days. Cornelius Priscus [erected this].
EDCS-29000049.summary: A tombstone inscription for Cornelia Prisca, detailing her age at death and erected by Cornelius Priscus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29000517.translation: To Gaius Dindius Blandus and Octavia Quarta, daughter of Publius, and to their son Gaius Dindius Blandus, aged 8 years.
EDCS-29000517.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Gaius Dindius Blandus, his wife Octavia Quarta, and their young son Gaius Dindius Blandus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100119.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus memoriae Numitoriae Proculae Cai Numitori Senecae veterani legionis VII Claudiae \n
EDCS-29100119.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for the memory of Numitoria Procula, [daughter] of Gaius Numitorius Seneca, a veteran of the VII Claudia legion.
EDCS-29100119.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Numitoria Procula, daughter of a veteran of the VII Claudia legion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100120.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Aebutio Luci filio Papiria Grato vixit annos LVI hic situs est Aebutia Marcia coniugi \n
EDCS-29100120.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Lucius Aebutius Gratus, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, lived 56 years, is buried here. Aebutia Marcia set this up for her husband.
EDCS-29100120.summary: A tombstone inscription for Lucius Aebutius Gratus, erected by his wife Aebutia Marcia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100121.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Valerio Aurelio Diocletiano Pio Felici Invicto Augsto \n
EDCS-29100121.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Aurelius Diocletianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-29100121.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Diocletian, dating to his reign (284-305 AD).

EDCS-29100122.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Aurelius Andronicus veteranus ex signifero legionis VII Claudiae vixit annos LIII Lucius Aurelius Salvianus librarius consularis filius\n
EDCS-29100122.translation: To the divine spirits. Lucius Aurelius Andronicus, a veteran and signifer of the Legio VII Claudia, lived 53 years. Lucius Aurelius Salvianus, a scribe for a consular official and his son, [dedicated this].
EDCS-29100122.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Aurelius Andronicus, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia, set up by his son.

EDCS-29100123.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Valerius Speratus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae ex beneficiario consularis decurio municipii Aeli Viminacii praefectus cohortis I Aquetanorum sic vixit annos LV obitus in Brittannia Lucia Afrodisia coniugi bene merenti et sibi vivae posuit\n
EDCS-29100123.translation: To the divine spirits. Marcus Valerius Speratus, a veteran of the Legio VII Claudia, former beneficiarius of a consular official, decurion of the municipium of Aelia Viminacium, and prefect of the Cohors I Aquetanorum, lived 55 years. Lucia Afrodisia placed this for her well-deserving husband and for herself while alive, after his death in Britannia.
EDCS-29100123.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Speratus, a veteran with a distinguished career, and his wife Lucia Afrodisia, likely set up by the wife.

EDCS-29100124.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Barbio Luci liberto Nymphodoto vixit nnos XVII Barbius Delphicus et Quarta parentes faciendum curaverunt hic situs est\n
EDCS-29100124.translation: To the divine spirits. Lucius Barbinius Nymphodotus, freedman of Lucius Barbinius, lived 17 years. Barbius Delphicus and Quarta, his parents, took care to have this made. He is buried here.
EDCS-29100124.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Barbinius Nymphodotus, a young freedman, dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-29100126.text_edition: \n Marcus Iulius Priscus filius et heres matri pientissimae bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29100126.translation: Marcus Iulius Priscus, his son and heir, placed this for his most dutiful mother, who deserved well.
EDCS-29100126.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed mother, dedicated by her son and heir.

EDCS-29100128.text_edition: \n vixit annos XX militavit menses VIII Publius Aurelius Marcianus miles legionis VII Marcianus veteranus legionis VII pater et Aurelia Marcia mater se viva sibi et filiis bene merentibus posuerunt Aurelia Rufina brutes\n
EDCS-29100128.translation: He lived 20 years and served 8 months. Publius Aurelius Marcianus, a soldier of Legio VII, Marcianus, a veteran of Legio VII. His father and Aurelia Marcia, his mother, placed this for themselves while alive and for their well-deserving sons. Aurelia Rufina [perhaps a daughter or relative].
EDCS-29100128.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aurelius Marcianus, a soldier and veteran of Legio VII, set up by his parents for him and their other sons.

EDCS-29100130.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus AGILES vixit nnos LX Valerius Victorinus legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos III Valeri-- PPM \n
EDCS-29100130.translation: To the divine spirits. Agiles lived 60 years. Valerius Victorinus [son of Agiles?] lived 3 years. Valeri-- PPM [perhaps parents or dedicators]
EDCS-29100130.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Agiles and possibly his young son Valerius Victorinus, with incomplete dedicatory information.

EDCS-29100131.text_edition: \n LO vixit nnos XXXVIII Marcus Aurelius Rufinianus fratri et Ulpia Navia coniunx eius et Aurelius Vitalis et VAIAV IIMRIVV\n
EDCS-29100131.translation: He lived 38 years. Marcus Aurelius Rufinianus. His brother and his wife Ulpia Navia, and Aurelius Vitalis and VAIAV IIMRIVV [likely other relatives or dedicators] [dedicated this].
EDCS-29100131.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Rufinianus, likely set up by his brother, wife, and other relatives.

EDCS-29100133.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo a Procnus miles cohortis I praetoriae \n
EDCS-29100133.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Procnus, a soldier of Cohors I Praetoriae [dedicated this].
EDCS-29100133.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus dedicated by Procnus, a soldier of the Praetorian Guard.

EDCS-29100134.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute domini nostri Augusti Marcus Aurelius Gaius miles legionis VII Claudiae strator consularis votum solvens libens merito posuit Imperatore [] et Comazonte consulibus\n
EDCS-29100134.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of our lord Augustus. Marcus Aurelius Gaius, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia, strator of a consular official, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly. Dedicated during the consulship of [] and Comazonte.
EDCS-29100134.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus dedicated by a soldier of Legio VII Claudia for the emperor's health during a specific consulship.

EDCS-29100443.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti et Iuliae Domnae Augustae matris Augusti et castrorum dedicante Lucio Mario Perpetuo consulari per Restutum magistrum civium Romanorum Dianae sacerdotes eiusdem loci dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-29100443.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Dolichenus, for the safety of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus and Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the emperor and of the camps. Dedicated by Lucius Marius Perpetuus, a consular official, through Restutus, magistrate of the Roman citizens; his priests of Diana dedicated this.
EDCS-29100443.summary: This is a complex votive inscription to Jupiter Dolichenus for the safety of emperors and empresses, dedicated by a consular official and priests.

EDCS-29601696.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Publius Aelius Valerianus vestiarius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29601696.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Publius Aelius Valerianus the clothier gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29601696.summary: A votive inscription to Mithras by Publius Aelius Valerianus, a clothier, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29601699.text_edition: \n diebus decessit hora nocturna VII Aurelius Felicianus praefectus legionis VII Claudiae coniugi dignissimae\n
EDCS-29601699.translation: Aurelius Felicianus, prefect of the VII Claudia legion, dedicated this to his most worthy wife, who died during the seventh night hour.
EDCS-29601699.summary: A funerary dedication by Aurelius Felicianus, a legionary prefect, to his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601700.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Pomponius Valerianus miles legionis VII Claudiae beneficiarius consularis vixit annos XXVIIII militavit annos X VA Pomponia soror eius \n
EDCS-29601700.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Caius Pomponius Valerianus, a soldier of the VII Claudia legion, a beneficiarius consularis, lived 29 years and served 10 years. His sister, Pomponia, [erected this].
EDCS-29601700.summary: A tombstone for Caius Pomponius Valerianus, a soldier and beneficiarius of the VII Claudia legion, erected by his sister, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601701.translation: [...a veteran of the legion, his brother]
EDCS-29601701.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, mentioning a veteran of a legion and his brother.

EDCS-29601703.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavius Dalmatius praepositus vicsit sic annis LX per instantiam Butiici Canis egit uriente L EI \n
EDCS-29601703.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Flavius Dalmatius, the praepositus, lived 60 years, through the urging of Buticius Canis, he administered...
EDCS-29601703.summary: A tombstone for Flavius Dalmatius, a praepositus, likely from the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-29700202.text_edition: \n Diti Patri et Proserpinae Reginae Quintus Sabinius Vitalis primus Augustalium\n
EDCS-29700202.translation: To Father Dis and Queen Proserpina, Quintus Sabinius Vitalis, first of the Augustales.
EDCS-29700202.summary: A dedication to Pluto and Proserpina by Quintus Sabinius Vitalis, a prominent local official (primus Augustalium), likely from a Roman provincial town.

EDCS-29700203.text_edition: \n Cereri Augustae Caius Severius Valens decurio et quaestor II VI municipii Aeli Viminacii ob honorem decurionatus posuit\n
EDCS-29700203.translation: Caius Severius Valens, decurio and quaestor II vir of the municipium Aeli Viminacii, set this up in honor of his office as decurio, to Ceres Augusta.
EDCS-29700203.summary: A dedication to Ceres Augusta by Caius Severius Valens, a local magistrate of Aelia Viminacium, marking his promotion to decurio.

EDCS-29700204.text_edition: \n Dianae Marcus Laelius Maximus legatus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis\n
EDCS-29700204.translation: To Diana, Marcus Laelius Maximus, legate of the pious and faithful VII Claudia legion.
EDCS-29700204.summary: A dedication to Diana by Marcus Laelius Maximus, a legate of the VII Claudia legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700205.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto Aelius Civilis miles legionis VII Claudiae centuria Aufidini Victoris primi pili hoc signum fecit\n
EDCS-29700205.translation: Aelius Civilis, a soldier of the VII Claudia legion, centuria of Aufidius Victor, primus pilus, made this statue to Hercules Augustus.
EDCS-29700205.summary: An offering to Hercules by Aelius Civilis, a distinguished soldier (primus pilus) of the VII Claudia legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700206.summary: A dedication to Hercules by Caius Julius Arrianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700207.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Fab votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-29700207.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Valerius Fab willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-29700207.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter, dedicated by Valerius Fab, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700208.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae decurio aedilis coloniae Viminacii ex voto libens merito posuit\n
EDCS-29700208.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, a decurion and aedile of the colony of Viminacium, willingly and deservedly set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-29700208.summary: A dedicatory inscription to Mithras by a local magistrate in the colony of Viminacium, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700209.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Antonini Augusti Pii et Veri Caesaris veterani legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis probati Serviano et Varo et Pontiano et Attico consulibus missi honesta missione per Curtium Iustum legatum Augusti pro praetore qui et legatum legionis et sunt numero CCXXXIX Ulpius Pharnax Valerius Maximus Sextius Maximus I Valerius Aquilinus beneficarius Silius Felix Aemilius Myrismus Passidenius Meri Valerius Maximus Aelius Marcellinus Publius Aelius Fortunatus s Valerius Valens Pupinius Latinus Longinius Alexander Aelius Longus Publius Aelius Augustinus cornicularius Aelius Aquilinus Iulius Longinus Aelius Maximus Aelius Festus cohors III Aelius Rufus Iulius Niarchus Lucius Valerius Proculus Aelius Apollonius Valerius Clemens Valerius Alexander Aelius Paulinus Aelius Marcianus Cornelius Flavianus Valerius Alexander cohors IIII Aelius Secundus Flavius Magnus Aelius Firmus Cornelius Rufus Valerius Crispus Flavius Quintus Aurelius Vale Aelius Marcianus \n
EDCS-29700209.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Antoninus Augustus Pius and Caesar Verus, veterans of the VII Claudia Pious Faithful legion, tested, under the consulship of Servianus, Varus, Pontianus, and Atticus, discharged with honor through Curtius Justus, imperial legate with praetorian power, who is also legate of the legion, and comprising 239 men: Ulpius Pharnax, Valerius Maximus, Sextius Maximus I, Valerius Aquilinus, Beneficarius, Silius Felix, Aemilius Myrismus, Passidenius Meri, Valerius Maximus, Aelius Marcellinus, Publius Aelius Fortunatus s, Valerius Valens, Pupinius Latinus, Longinius Alexander, Aelius Longus, Publius Aelius Augustinus, Cornicularius, Aelius Aquilinus, Iulius Longinus, Aelius Maximus, Aelius Festus, Cohort III, Aelius Rufus, Iulius Niarchus, Lucius Valerius Proculus, Aelius Apollonius, Valerius Clemens, Valerius Alexander, Aelius Paulinus, Aelius Marcianus, Cornelius Flavianus, Valerius Alexander, Cohort IIII, Aelius Secundus, Flavius Magnus, Aelius Firmus, Cornelius Rufus, Valerius Crispus, Flavius Quintus, Aurelius Vale, Aelius Marcianus.
EDCS-29700209.summary: An inscription recording the names of 239 veterans of the VII Claudia legion who were granted honorable discharge, dating to the consulship of Servianus, Varus, Pontianus, and Atticus (likely AD 151 or 165-166).

EDCS-29700212.text_edition: \n pro salute Marciae Otaciliae Severae sanctissimae Augustae coniugis domini nostri Phillip<p>i Augusti et matris Philippi nobilissimi Caesaris et castrorum domum suam et balneum refecit et paravit Tiberius Claudius Marcellinus eques Romanus dec IIII Marc- Aurel- Un dec V \n
EDCS-29700212.translation: For the safety of Marcia Otacilia Severa, most holy Augusta, wife of our lord Philip Augustus, and mother of the most noble Caesar Philip, Tiberius Claudius Marcellinus, a Roman knight of the IVth cohort, and Marcus Aurelius Un, of the Vth cohort, repaired and prepared their house and baths.
EDCS-29700212.summary: A dedication for the safety of Empress Otacilia Severa and Prince Philip, recording the repair of a house and bath by two Roman knights, likely from the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-29700213.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Antiochus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae vixit annos LX et Publius Aelius Donatus alumnus eius vixit annos VII Publius Aelius Agathangelus et Publius Aelius Primitivos sic liberti et heredes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-29700213.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Publius Aelius Antiochus, a veteran of the VII Claudia legion, lived 60 years, and Publius Aelius Donatus, his freedman protégé, lived 7 years. Publius Aelius Agathangelus and Publius Aelius Primitivos, freedmen and heirs, saw to the construction.
EDCS-29700213.summary: A funerary inscription for a veteran of the VII Claudia legion and his freedman protégé, set up by freedmen and heirs, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700214.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Priscus miles legionis VII Claudiae stipendiorum XXV vixit annos L Publius Aelius Ursulus filius et Publius Aelius Capito libertus heredes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-29700214.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Publius Aelius Priscus, a soldier of the VII Claudia legion, served 25 years, lived 50 years. Publius Aelius Ursulus, his son, and Publius Aelius Capito, his freedman, heirs, saw to the construction.
EDCS-29700214.summary: A funerary inscription for a soldier of the VII Claudia legion, set up by his son and freedman heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700215.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Marcianus stator legati legionis VII Claudiae stipendiorum XXVI Cutius annorum XIII Aprilis annorum XII IVLISRISCRIANI Apria stipendiorum X Aurelia Cutia coniugi et filis et nepoti et Helvius Octavianus nepos heres avunculo et contubernalibus posuerunt Antiochiano et Orfito consulibus\n
EDCS-29700215.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelius Marcianus, *stator* of the legate of the VII Claudia legion, served 26 years, aged 13 years. Cutius, 12 years. Apria, served 10 years. Aurelia Cutia, for her husband, sons, and grandson, and Helvius Octavianus, grandson and heir, for his uncle and tent-mates, set this up under the consulship of Antiochianus and Orfitus.
EDCS-29700215.summary: A funerary inscription for soldiers and a *stator* of the VII Claudia legion, with familial dedications, dating to the consulship of Antiochianus and Orfitus (likely AD 171).

EDCS-29700216.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Rufus tesserarius legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos XL militavit nnos XX Aurelius Longinus et Aurelius Aulutralis milites legionis eiustem sic conheredes bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-29700216.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Aurelius Rufus, *tesserarius* of the VII Claudia legion, lived 40 years, served 20 years. Aurelius Longinus and Aurelius Aulutralis, soldiers of the same legion, as co-heirs, set this up for him who deserved well.
EDCS-29700216.summary: A funerary inscription for a *tesserarius* of the VII Claudia legion, set up by his co-heir fellow soldiers, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700217.text_edition: \n C(aio)? Domitio Cai filio riculo custodi armorum legionis VII Claudiae vixit annis XXXXII et Neriae Claudiae vixit annis XXVII coniugi eius Domitia Anthusa mater et Domitii Digna Potentinus Ingenua Potentina Surrentus filii et Domitius Iulius nepos bene merentibus posuerunt\n
EDCS-29700217.translation: To Gaius Domitius Riculus, son of Gaius, guardian of arms of the VII Claudia legion, lived 42 years, and to Neria Claudia, lived 27 years, his wife. Domitia Anthusa, his mother, and Domitii Digna, Potentinus, Ingenua, Potentina, Surrentus, his children, and Domitius Iulius, his grandson, set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29700217.summary: A funerary inscription for a soldier of the VII Claudia legion and his wife, set up by his mother, children, and grandson, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700218.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Helvius Probus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos LIII hic situs est Pontia Optata coniugi suo pientissimo bene merenti posuit Quintus Helvius Optatus patri suo bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29700218.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Helvius Probus, a veteran of the VII Claudia legion, lived 53 years. He is buried here. Pontia Optata set this up for her most devoted and deserving husband. Quintus Helvius Optatus set this up for his deserving father.
EDCS-29700218.summary: A funerary inscription for a veteran of the VII Claudia legion, with dedications from his wife and son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700220.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Paternio Titi filio Ianuario veterano legionis IIII Flaviae et Iuniae Ianuariae Paternia Ianuaria filia et heres II V A \n
EDCS-29700220.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Lucius Paternus Ianuarius, son of Titus, a veteran of the Fourth Legion Flavia, and for Iunia Ianuaria. Paternus Ianuarius, daughter and heir, [erected this].
EDCS-29700220.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated to Lucius Paternus Ianuarius, a veteran of Legio IV Flavia, and his daughter Iunia Ianuaria, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700221.text_edition: \n IVSAIII faciend(um) curavit Caius Terentius Cai filius Claudia Catullus Viruno veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis ex signifero contirunculo et contubernali pientissimo Crispino et Iuliano II consulibus\n
EDCS-29700221.translation: Caius Terentius Catullus, son of Caius, a veteran from Virunum of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, a standard-bearer and comrade, had this made for his most devoted companion and tent-mate Crispinius and Iulianus, under the second consulship.
EDCS-29700221.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument by Caius Terentius Catullus, a veteran and standard-bearer of Legio VII Claudia, for his devoted comrade Crispinius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700222.text_edition: \n manus veteranus legionis vixit annos hic situs est Titus Flavius legionis eiusdem heres bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29700222.translation: A veteran of the legion lived for [X] years. Here lies Titus Flavius [of the same legion], his heir erected [this] for one who deserved well.
EDCS-29700222.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Titus Flavius, a veteran of an unnamed legion, whose heir placed the monument, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700223.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Valerio Valeriano no VL militavit annos XVI RVI f\n
EDCS-29700223.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Caius Valerius Valerianus, who served in the [X] legion for 16 years...
EDCS-29700223.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone inscription for Caius Valerius Valerianus, a soldier who served 16 years in an unnamed legion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700224.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Lucilius ET <f=E>ilius Herculanus decurio aedilis municipii Aeli Viminacii vixit nnos XXXIII Claudia Hygia marito bene merenti \n
EDCS-29700224.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Lucius Lucilius Herculanus, son of Lucius, a decurion and aedile of the municipium of Aelia Viminacium, who lived for 33 years. Claudia Hygia [erected this] for her deserving husband.
EDCS-29700224.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Lucilius Herculanus, a local official in Aelia Viminacium, set up by his wife Claudia Hygia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700225.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sexto Valerio Valenti decurioni municipii Aeli Viminacii vixit annos LXIII Valerii Primus Herculianus Valentinus heredes patri pientissimo bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-29700225.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Sextus Valerius Valens, decurion of the municipium of Aelia Viminacium, who lived for 63 years. His heirs, the Valerii Primus Herculianus and Valentinus, placed [this] for their most devoted father, who deserved well.
EDCS-29700225.summary: This tombstone inscription honors Sextus Valerius Valens, a decurion of Aelia Viminacium, erected by his sons and heirs, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700226.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Felici qui vixit nnis diebus XV et iano qui vixit annis diebus XXIII mas Augustalis col(oniae) s et Vibia \n
EDCS-29700226.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Felix, who lived 15 days, and for Ianus, who lived 23 days. Augustalis of the colony, and Vibia...
EDCS-29700226.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone inscription recording the short lives of Felix and Ianus, possibly children, with a dedication by Augustalis and Vibia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700227.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Theodotae rarissimae feminae quae vixit annis XXXI mensibus IIII diebus XVII Aurelius Gallicus coniugi bene de se meritae\n
EDCS-29700227.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelia Theodota, a most rare woman, who lived for 31 years, 4 months, and 17 days. Aurelius Gallicus [erected this] for his wife, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-29700227.summary: This tombstone inscription commemorates Aurelia Theodota, who lived 31 years, 4 months, and 17 days, erected by her husband Aurelius Gallicus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700228.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Rufina Valentis vixit annis VIIII Mir Rufi mater et Valens Geti pater bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-29700228.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Rufina, daughter of Valens, who lived 9 years. Mir[X], mother of Rufi, and Valens Geti, father, made [this] for [her] who deserved well.
EDCS-29700228.summary: This tombstone inscription is for Rufina, daughter of Valens and Mir[X], and granddaughter of Valens Geti, who died at age 9, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700229.text_edition: \n Valerius et Marcus Valerius Barbario heredes bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-29700229.translation: Valerius and Marcus Valerius Barbarus, his heirs, placed [this] for [him] who deserved well.
EDCS-29700229.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording that Valerius and Marcus Valerius Barbarus, as heirs, erected a monument for a deserving individual, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700230.text_edition: \n in terra quam procul a patria moribus et forma pariter laudabilis ut quis cernere non posset pulchrior an melior nunc tumulus super et superest Petronia nomen annos bis denos adque duos tetuli mariti qui caruit vita quom sic caret hac anima nec carum cinerem at sic terram asportare paternam quivit et hic miserum ut discruciet stimulus\n
EDCS-29700230.translation: In the earth, far from her homeland, equally praiseworthy in character and appearance, so that no one could see if she were more beautiful or better, now a tomb rests above, and Petronia, wife of the husband who lacked life for twenty-two years, endures. Since she is without this soul, and he could not carry his dear ashes to his ancestral land, and here, to torment the wretched, a sting [remains].
EDCS-29700230.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Petronia, recording her virtue, her marriage of 22 years, and her husband's inability to bring her remains back to their homeland.

EDCS-29700231.text_edition: \n nus et Gaius Iulius Marcellinus matri bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-29700231.translation: Gaius and Gaius Julius Marcellinus set up [this monument] for their well-deserving mother.
EDCS-29700231.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Gaius and Gaius Julius Marcellinus to their mother.

EDCS-29700232.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records an official, a duumvir, of a municipality named Augustalis.

EDCS-29700233.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et perpetuae securitati Cocceius Cocceianus vixit nnos L et Cocceia Sabina vixit nnos V Aemilia Ma coniux et Coccei- \n
EDCS-29700233.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to eternal security. Cocceius Cocceianus lived 50 years and Cocceia Sabina lived 5 years. Aemilia Ma, wife of Coccei-
EDCS-29700233.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cocceius Cocceianus and Cocceia Sabina, noting their ages and begun by Aemilia Ma, likely their relative or wife.

EDCS-29700234.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, beginning with the name Gaius Rufus.

EDCS-29700235.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Sextus Alpinius Alcimus ornatus orrnamentis decurionalibus municipii Aureli Augusti Margi et Lucius Valerius Trofimas Augustalis municipii supra scripti\n
EDCS-29700235.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Sextus Alpinius Alcimus, honored with the decurional ornaments of the Aurelian municipality of Augustus of Margi, and Lucius Valerius Trofimas, Augustalis of the aforementioned municipality
EDCS-29700235.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Sextus Alpinius Alcimus and Lucius Valerius Trofimas, both officials in the municipality of Margi.

EDCS-29700236.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Antonius Narcissus de suo posuit et porticus fecit\n
EDCS-29700236.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Antonius Narcissus erected [this monument] at his own expense and built a portico.
EDCS-29700236.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Antonius Narcissus, who also funded the construction of a portico.

EDCS-29700240.text_edition: \n Deo Heroni collitores ipsius Theodotus Gude pater Victorinus Mucianus Valentinus Rodo Natus Victorinus Dometianus sic Septuminus sic Zinama Herodes Hermogenes Iulius Hermogenes Maximinus Marcus votum posuerunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-29700240.translation: To the god Heron, his associates Theodotus, son of Gude, Victorinus Mucianus, Valentinus Rodo, Natus Victorinus, Dometianus, Septimius, Zinama, Herodes, Hermogenes, Julius Hermogenes, Maximinus, Marcus, made a vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-29700240.summary: This is a votive offering to the god Heron made by a group of individuals, possibly associates or villagers.

EDCS-29700241.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Aurelius Marcus |(centurio) legionis IIII Flaviae Gallienae ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-29700241.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Paterno Aurelius Marcus, centurion of the IV Flavia Galliena legion, set up [this monument] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-29700241.summary: This is a military dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Paterno Aurelius Marcus, a centurion of the IV Flavia Galliena legion, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29700242.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Victorino equiti Romano vixit nnos V diem uno sic Iulius Florianus |(centurio) legionis IIII Flaviae et Victorina filio karissimo\n
EDCS-29700242.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Victorinus, a Roman knight, lived 5 years and 1 day. Thus Iulius Florianus, centurion of the IV Flavia legion, and Victorina [set this up] for their dearest son.
EDCS-29700242.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Victorinus, a Roman knight, set up by his father, Iulius Florianus, a centurion, and his mother, Victorina.

EDCS-29700243.text_edition: \n Nymphis salutaribus Catius Celer pontifexs sic Lucilla coniuge et Lucio et Sexsto sic filiis\n
EDCS-29700243.translation: To the health-giving Nymphs, Catius Celer, the priest, with his wife Lucilla and sons Lucius and Sextus.
EDCS-29700243.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Nymphs by a priest, his wife, and sons, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700244.text_edition: \n Dis et Deabus sacrum Caius Sentius Primus IIIIIIvir Augustalis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29700244.translation: Sacred to the Gods and Goddesses. Gaius Sentius Primus, a college member of the Augustales priests, willingly paid his vow, deservedly.
EDCS-29700244.summary: This is a votive inscription to the gods by Gaius Sentius Primus, a prominent civic official, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29700245.summary: A dedication to the goddess Fortuna by a woman named Betuvia Respecta, probably from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29700247.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Cassius Celer aedilis decurio quaestor IIvir augur IIvir quinquennalis coloniae Scupinorum\n
EDCS-29700247.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Gaius Cassius Celer, aedile, decurion, quaestor, duovir, augur, and quinquennial duovir of the colony of Scupi.
EDCS-29700247.summary: This is an honorary inscription listing the numerous civic and religious offices held by Gaius Cassius Celer in the Roman colony of Scupi, likely dating to the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29700248.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Caius Lucilius Valerianus aram libens posuit\n
EDCS-29700248.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Gaius Lucilius Valerianus willingly set up the altar.
EDCS-29700248.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus by Gaius Lucilius Valerianus, who willingly erected the altar, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29700250.text_edition: \n Invicto Imperatori Pio Felici Gallieno Augusto dis animo voltuque compari res publica\n
EDCS-29700250.translation: To the Unconquered Emperor Pius Felix Gallienus Augustus, the state, with a like spirit and disposition.
EDCS-29700250.summary: This is an inscription by the state dedicating something to Emperor Gallienus, likely from his reign (253-268 CE).

EDCS-29700252.text_edition: \n D(is)? M(anibus) us us nus us Aelius legionis vixit hic situs est ANI filio bene merenti posuit Marcus Aurelius Mestrius veteranus legionis VII Claudiae vixit annis LXXX Marcus Aurelius Mestrianus filius patri et filio posuit\n
EDCS-29700252.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius [---], of the legion [---], lived here and is buried. Marcus Aurelius Mestrius, a veteran of the VII Claudia legion, lived 80 years and is buried here. His son, Marcus Aurelius Mestrianus, erected this for his father and for his son.
EDCS-29700252.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aelius and Marcus Aurelius Mestrius, erected by the latter's son, Marcus Aurelius Mestrianus, a veteran of the VII Claudia legion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700253.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Dindius Marcellus cornicularius legati legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos XL hic situs est quo<d=T> testamento fieri praeceperat famula Parthenope n eris sic faciendum curavit quod opus est Idibus Augustis Aeliano consulibus \n
EDCS-29700253.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Dindius Marcellus, *cornicularius* of the legate of the VII Claudia legion, lived 40 years and is buried here, as he had ordered to be done in his will. His slave Parthenope took care to have it done. This work was completed on the Ides of August in the consulship of Aelianus.
EDCS-29700253.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Titus Dindius Marcellus, a military officer of the VII Claudia legion, who died at 40 and was buried according to his will, with the work completed in 144 CE.

EDCS-29700255.text_edition: \n Marcus Licinius Marci filius Voltinia Luco Optatus veteranus legionis I Italicae m[ilitavit ------\n
EDCS-29700255.translation: Marcus Licinius Luco Optatus, son of Marcus Licinius, of the Voltinia tribe, a veteran of the I Italica legion, served [---].
EDCS-29700255.summary: This is a fragmented inscription recording the name and military service of Marcus Licinius Luco Optatus, a veteran of the I Italica legion, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29700256.text_edition: \n Quintus Petronius Marci filius Scaptia Rufus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis deducticius titulum fieri iussit\n
EDCS-29700256.translation: Quintus Petronius Rufus, son of Marcus Petronius, of the Scaptia tribe, a veteran of the VII Claudia legion, loyal and faithful, a *deducticius*, ordered this memorial to be made.
EDCS-29700256.summary: This inscription records Quintus Petronius Rufus, a veteran of the VII Claudia legion and a *deducticius*, who ordered this memorial to be erected, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29700257.text_edition: \n Caius Rauconius Cai filius Scaptiae Verecundus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis militavit in signis nnos XXXII deductus deductione vixit nnos LXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-29700257.translation: Gaius Rauconius Verecundus, son of Gaius, of the Scaptia tribe, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, served for 32 years and was discharged; he lived for 70 years and is buried here.
EDCS-29700257.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Rauconius Verecundus, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, recording his service and age at death, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-29700258.text_edition: \n Caius Vibius Arator miles frumentarius legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis militavit nnos XVII vixit nnos XXXX hic situs est Iulia Obulcia filio piissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-29700258.translation: Gaius Vibius Arator, a supply soldier of the Seventh Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, served for 17 years and lived for 40 years and is buried here. Iulia Obulcia had this made for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-29700258.summary: This tombstone commemorates Gaius Vibius Arator, a supply soldier of the Seventh Claudian Legion, with his service and age at death, erected by his mother Iulia Obulcia, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-29700259.text_edition: \n N legionis IIII arius fabricae legionis eiusdem vixit annis LXV hic situs est Obellia Prima uxor bene merenti faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-29700259.translation: [...] of the Second [Fourth?] Legion, a craftsman of the same legion, lived for 65 years and is buried here. Obellia Prima, his wife, had this made for her deserving husband.
EDCS-29700259.summary: This tombstone records the burial of an unnamed craftsman from the Second [Fourth?] Legion, who lived to 65, and was commemorated by his wife Obellia Prima.

EDCS-29700260.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sextus Caelidius Secundus Sexti Caelidi Lupi et Mataurae Cominiae fili<us=O> Aemilia Stob<i=O>s cui ordo coloniae Scupinorum honores aedilitatis et decurionatus contulit vixit annos XVIII dies XXXX sic hic situs est \n
EDCS-29700260.translation: To the departed spirits. Sextus Caelidius Secundus, son of Sextus Caelidius Lupus and Cominia Mataura. The town council of Scupinorum bestowed upon him the honors of the aedileship and the decurionate; he lived for 18 years and 40 days and is buried here. Aemilia Stobios [had this made?].
EDCS-29700260.summary: This funerary inscription honors Sextus Caelidius Secundus, a young man who received civic honors from the colony of Scupinorum before his death at age 18, likely commissioned by Aemilia Stobios.

EDCS-29700261.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Ulpius Iulius Augustalis coloniae Scupinorum vixit annos LXXVI hic situs est Suillia Firmina marito bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29700261.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Iulius Augustalis of the colony of Scupinorum, lived for 76 years and is buried here. Suillia Firmina placed this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-29700261.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Marcus Ulpius Iulius Augustalis, a resident of the colony of Scupinorum, who lived to 76, and was commemorated by his wife Suillia Firmina.

EDCS-29700262.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Suestius aedili<s=C> vixit annos XXXIIII Caius Valerius Claudius filius eius vixit nnos IIII hic siti sunt Claudia Gallita \n
EDCS-29700262.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Valerius Suestius, an aedile, lived for 34 years. Gaius Valerius Claudius, his son, lived for 4 years. They are buried here. Claudia Gallita [had this made?].
EDCS-29700262.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaius Valerius Suestius, an aedile who died at 34, and his young son Gaius Valerius Claudius who died at 4, likely commissioned by Claudia Gallita.

EDCS-29700263.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus tha vixit annos XXXII hic sita est ONON OLON monumentum faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-29700263.translation: To the departed spirits. [...] lived for 32 years and is buried here. ONON OLON had this monument made.
EDCS-29700263.summary: This partially preserved funerary inscription records the burial of an individual who lived for 32 years, with the monument commissioned by someone named ONON OLON.

EDCS-29700264.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Alexander vixit annis XVIIII Caties Alexandri filio pientissimo posuit\n
EDCS-29700264.translation: To the departed spirits. Alexander lived for 19 years. Caties placed this for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-29700264.summary: This tombstone commemorates Alexander, who died at the age of 19, and was set up by his mother Caties.

EDCS-29700265.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Ambibia Maria, recording only her name and the beginning of her lifespan.

EDCS-29700266.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caelidia Secunda vixit annos L hic sita est Claudius Herculanus maritus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29700266.translation: To the departed spirits. Caelidia Secunda lived for 50 years and is buried here. Claudius Herculanus, her husband, placed this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-29700266.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Caelidia Secunda, who died at 50, and was commemorated by her husband Claudius Herculanus.

EDCS-29700267.text_edition: \n Catinia Catini liberta Festa vixit nnos XXX hic sita est Marcus Catinius Crispus libertae et coniugi pientissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-29700267.translation: Festa, freedwoman of Catinia, lived 30 years and is buried here. Marcus Catinius Crispus had this made for his freedwoman and most devoted wife.
EDCS-29700267.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Festa, a freedwoman, set up by her husband Marcus Catinius Crispus, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700268.text_edition: \n Claudia Ingenua Claudi Secularis filia vixit annos XXXX hic sita est Caius Iu<l=I>(ius) Val coniugi bene merenti <f=E>(aciendum) curavit \n
EDCS-29700268.translation: Claudia Ingenua, daughter of Claudius Secularis, lived 40 years and is buried here. Caius Iulius Val had this made for his deserving wife.
EDCS-29700268.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Ingenua, set up by her husband Caius Iulius Val, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700269.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Claudius Ursio vixit nnos LV hic situs est Valeria Lysimache marito bene merenti cum Urso filio faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-29700269.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Claudius Ursio lived 55 years and is buried here. Valeria Lysimache had this made, along with their son Urso, for her deserving husband.
EDCS-29700269.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Claudius Ursio, set up by his wife Valeria Lysimache and son Urso, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700270.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Luci libertus domo Methymneus vixit annos LXXV \n
EDCS-29700270.translation: Lucius Cornelius, freedman of Lucius, from Methymna, lived 75 years.
EDCS-29700270.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Lucius Cornelius, a freedman from Methymna, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700271.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Euhelpis [lived...]
EDCS-29700271.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Euhelpis, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700272.text_edition: \n Ianuaria vixit annos XXXV hic sita est XP \n
EDCS-29700272.translation: Ianuaria lived 35 years and is buried here. [Cross symbol]
EDCS-29700272.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ianuaria, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700273.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia vixit nnos XXX hic sita est Soda Celeris <f=E>(ilia) faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-29700273.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia lived 30 years and is buried here. Soda Celeris, daughter of [---], had this made.
EDCS-29700273.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia, set up by Soda Celeris, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700274.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Glauci [lived...]
EDCS-29700274.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Caius Iulius Glauci, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700275.text_edition: \n Muc Neri liberta vixit annis XX hic sita est Quintus Nerius Fortunatus libertae et coniugi bene merenti de suo titulum faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-29700275.translation: Muc, freedwoman of Neri, lived 20 years and is buried here. Quintus Nerius Fortunatus had this monument made from his own funds for his deserving freedwoman and wife.
EDCS-29700275.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Muc, a freedwoman, set up by Quintus Nerius Fortunatus, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700276.text_edition: \n Caius Scribonius Myras vixit annos VII hic situs est Caius Iulius Varenus \n
EDCS-29700276.translation: Caius Scribonius Myras lived 7 years and is buried here. Caius Iulius Varenus [had this made].
EDCS-29700276.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Scribonius Myras, likely set up by Caius Iulius Varenus, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700277.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Herculanus vixit annos LX hic situs est Maria Munatia faciendum curavit Maria Munatia vixit annos LX viva sibi et marito faciendum curavit et Valerius Fortunatus libertus commun<i=E>s fecit \n
EDCS-29700277.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Valerius Herculanus lived for 60 years; he is buried here. Maria Munatia saw to its construction. Maria Munatia lived for 60 years and saw to its construction while alive, for herself and her husband, and Valerius Fortunatus, their freedman, made it.
EDCS-29700277.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Herculanus and Maria Munatia, likely a married couple, with a freedman involved in its construction, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700278.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Varronius Urbanus vixit annos XLVI Cornelia Secunda coniugi bene merenti posuit Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelia Secunda vixit annos LX viva se posuit Publius Aelius Firmus et Varroni Maximus et Valens et Firmina fili matri bene merenti \n
EDCS-29700278.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Varronius Urbanus lived for 46 years. Cornelia Secunda placed this for her deserving husband. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cornelia Secunda lived for 60 years and placed this while alive for Publius Aelius Firmus, and for Varronius Maximus and Valens and Firmina, her deserving children and mother.
EDCS-29700278.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Lucius Varronius Urbanus and Cornelia Secunda, with the latter dedicating the monument for her family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700279.text_edition: \n hic situs est Caius Valerius Valens et Iulia Valeria fratri faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-29700279.translation: Caius Valerius Valens and Iulia Valeria saw to its construction for their brother.
EDCS-29700279.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating a brother, whose construction was overseen by his siblings Caius Valerius Valens and Iulia Valeria, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700280.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Maximus vixit annos L hic situs est Valerius Euporus qui et Maximus filius et Lucius Manlius Valentinus C et Servie viv<i=E> bene merenti faciendum curaverunt \n
EDCS-29700280.translation: To the Divine Shades. Valerius Maximus lived for 50 years; he is buried here. Valerius Euporus, also called Maximus, his son, and Lucius Manlius Valentinus, and Servius, saw to its construction for their deserving wife/mother and companion.
EDCS-29700280.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Valerius Maximus and was erected by his son Valerius Euporus (also called Maximus) and others, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700281.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpia Marcia vixit nnos XXXVIII Caius Sentius Primus uxori bene merenti\n
EDCS-29700281.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ulpia Marcia lived for 38 years. Caius Sentius Primus for his deserving wife.
EDCS-29700281.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpia Marcia, erected by her husband Caius Sentius Primus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700291.translation: To the Divine Shades. L. Alexandrio, freedman of CGSIA.
EDCS-29700291.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph for L. Alexandrio, a freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700292.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae sanctissimae ceterisque diis deabusquae omnibus sacrum Marcus Aurelius Titianus beneficiarius consularis legionis VII Claudiae votum solvit libens merito Caio Aufidio Victorino Tiberio Claudio Proculo consulibus\n
EDCS-29700292.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Regina, the most holy Minerva, and all other gods and goddesses. Marcus Aurelius Titianus, beneficiarius of a consul of the VII Claudia legion, gladly paid his vow according to his deserts, when Gaius Aufidius Victorinus and Tiberius Claudius Proculus were consuls.
EDCS-29700292.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Capitoline Triad and other deities by Marcus Aurelius Titianus, a soldier, during the consulship of Victorinus and Proculus, likely in the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-29700293.text_edition: \n Iovi et Iunoni et Dracconi sic et Draccenae sic et Alexandro Epitynchanus servus Furi Octaviani clarissimi viri posuit\n
EDCS-29700293.translation: To Jupiter and Juno, and thus to Dracco, and thus to Draccena, and thus to Alexander. Epitynchanus, a slave of the very distinguished man Furius Octavianus, placed this.
EDCS-29700293.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter, Juno, and other named figures, set up by a slave named Epitynchanus, belonging to Furius Octavianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700295.text_edition: \n Dominae Reginae et Domino et Bono Evento sic pro salute Augusti Ulpius Martinus strator consularis legionis VII Claudiae Severianae Alexandrianae votum posuit libens merito\n
EDCS-29700295.translation: To the Lady Regina, and to the Lord, and to Good Fortune, thus for the safety of Augustus. Ulpius Martinus, strator of a consul of the VII Claudia Severiana Alexandriana legion, gladly set up this vow according to his deserts.
EDCS-29700295.summary: This is a votive inscription for the safety of the emperor Augustus, dedicated by Ulpius Martinus, a soldier, to the goddesses Regina and Bono Evento, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700296.text_edition: \n Deae Iunoni Atilius Felicianus votum posuit\n
EDCS-29700296.translation: To the Goddess Juno. Atilius Felicianus set up his vow.
EDCS-29700296.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Juno by Atilius Felicianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29700297.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Dometianus sic ex voto suscepto posuit\n
EDCS-29700297.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Valerius Dometianus erected this according to his vow.
EDCS-29700297.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Valerius Dometianus.

EDCS-29700298.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo L Aurelius Vitalis ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus pro \n
EDCS-29700298.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, L. Aurelius Vitalis, adorned with decurional ornaments...
EDCS-29700298.summary: This is a partial dedication to Jupiter by L. Aurelius Vitalis, mentioning his civic status.

EDCS-29700299.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute domini nostri Augusti Titus Aurelius Atta miles legionis IIII Flaviae strator consularis posuit\n
EDCS-29700299.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Aurelius Atta, soldier of the IV Flavian legion and consular adjutant, erected this for the health of our lord Augustus.
EDCS-29700299.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by a soldier for the emperor's health.

EDCS-29700300.text_edition: \n Gaius Iulius Plato miles cohortis I Cilicum vixit annos XL militavit [annos ------\n
EDCS-29700300.translation: Gaius Julius Plato, soldier of the I Cohort of Cilicians, lived 40 years, served...
EDCS-29700300.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Julius Plato, a soldier, recording his age and length of service.

EDCS-29700301.text_edition: \n Gaius Iulius Severus miles cohortis I Aureliae Dardanorum vixit annos XLVII Iulius Varanus filio pientissimo --- \n
EDCS-29700301.translation: Gaius Julius Severus, soldier of the I Aurelian Cohort of Dardani, lived 47 years. Julius Varanus erected this for his most devoted son...
EDCS-29700301.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Julius Severus, a soldier, erected by his father.

EDCS-29700302.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Iulianus miles legionis VII Claudiae beneficiarius consularis vixit nnos XXXX Valeria BRAE sibi et coniugi Valerius Iulius et Valeria Iuliana patri bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-29700302.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Valerius Iulianus, soldier of the VII Claudian legion, a beneficiarius of a consul, lived 40 years. Valeria Brae, Valerius Julius and Valeria Iuliana erected this for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-29700302.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Valerius Iulianus, a soldier, erected by his family.

EDCS-29700303.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Cocceius Sextillianus sic decurio municipii Margi vixit nnos XXVI menses V Valeria Licinia coniux marito desideratissimo et Cocceiae Colendae filiae dulcissimae et sibi sequiturae viva faciendum curavit monumentum\n
EDCS-29700303.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Cocceius Sextillianus, decurion of the municipality of Margus, lived 26 years, 5 months. Valeria Licinia, his wife, had this monument made for her most desired husband, their daughter Cocceia Colenda, and herself.
EDCS-29700303.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Cocceius Sextillianus, a decurion, erected by his wife and daughter.

EDCS-29700305.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus NARIIALICAN IVISV memoria sic posuit\n
EDCS-29700305.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Narrialiicanivis erected this in memory...
EDCS-29700305.summary: This is a fragmented tombstone inscription dedicating memory to the Spirits of the Departed.

EDCS-29700306.text_edition: \n VICRA X militavit nnos XIIII Aurelia Faustina marito carissimo et generi sic pientissimo et Aurelia filia patri bene merenti et marito dulcissimo et sibi viva sua pecunia\n
EDCS-29700306.translation: served 14 years. Aurelia Faustina erected this with her own money for her most beloved husband, her most devoted son-in-law, and Aurelia, her daughter, for her well-deserving father, her sweetest husband, and for herself.
EDCS-29700306.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording service length and a complex family dedication.

EDCS-29700308.text_edition: \n Herculi Naisati Claudia Plotina ex voto deo posuit\n
EDCS-29700308.translation: To Hercules Naisatus, Claudia Plotina erected this according to her vow.
EDCS-29700308.summary: This is a votive dedication to Hercules Naisatus by Claudia Plotina.

EDCS-29700309.text_edition: \n Lucius Vecilius Cai filius Lemonia Modestus equo publico de quinque decuriis sevir aedilis duovir iure dicundo coloniae Hispellatium quaestor II augur praefectus fabrum tribunus militum legionis VI ferratae in Syria praefectus cohortis I Thracum Syriacae in Moesia equitatae cohors I Thracum Syriaca praefecto suo\n
EDCS-29700309.translation: Lucius Vecilius Modestus, son of Caius, of the Lemonia tribe, a public eques from the five decuries, former seviral official, aedile, duovir for pronouncing judgment, quaestor, augur, prefect of the fabrum, military tribune of the VI Ferrata legion in Syria, prefect of the I Thracum Syriaca cohort (equitated) in Moesia, [served] under his prefect.
EDCS-29700309.summary: This is a senatorial career inscription for Lucius Vecilius Modestus of Hispellum, dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29700310.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Sergius Paulus veteranus cohortis I Thracum Syriacae ex equite hic situs est vixit annos LXX vivo se sibi et Pupae coniugi bene merenti posuit servate nepotes\n
EDCS-29700310.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Sergius Paulus, a veteran of the I Thracum Syriaca cohort, formerly an equite, is buried here; he lived 70 years. While alive, he dedicated this to himself and to Pupa, his deserving wife. Respect your grandchildren.
EDCS-29700310.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Sergius Paulus, a veteran of the I Thracum Syriaca cohort, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29700311.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Augusti liberto Aprioni Augustali coloniae Ratiariae hic situs est vixit annos LXV Consia Valeria se viva sibi et coniugi suo cum Publis Aelis Proculo Avito decurionibus coloniae Ratiariae et Publis Aelis Aproniano Valente Proculliano filiis bene merentibus posuit\n
EDCS-29700311.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Aprionus, freedman of August, an Augustalis of the colony of Ratiaria, is buried here; he lived 65 years. Consia Valeria, while alive, dedicated this to herself and her deserving husband, along with Publius Aelius Proculus Avitus and Publius Aelius Apronianus Valens Procullianus, decurions of the colony of Ratiaria and her sons.
EDCS-29700311.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Aprionus, an Augustalis of Ratiaria, set up by his wife Consia Valeria, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700313.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription recording a dedication by Tertinius Herculanus, the rest of which is lost.

EDCS-29700314.text_edition: \n ------ qui] vixit nnos interfectus a stationariis cum Diurpagisa filio suo qui vixit nnos XVIII bene merentibus posuit\n
EDCS-29700314.translation: [...who] lived X years, was killed by guards together with his son Diurpagisa, who lived X years. [This was dedicated] by [someone] for the deserving.
EDCS-29700314.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the violent death of a father and son, possibly a mile marker or funerary monument, from an uncertain period.

EDCS-29700315.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Publio Liciinnio Valeriano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli et Imperatori Caesari Publio Linvi<c=O>inio Gall<e=F>nio sic Pio Felici Invicto Augusto \n
EDCS-29700315.translation: To Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and to Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-29700315.summary: This is a dedication honoring the Emperors Valerian and Gallienus, likely for a public building or monument, dated between 253 and 260 CE.

EDCS-29700316.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo patri patriae tribunicia potestate consuli proconsuli et Marciae Otaciliae Severae sanctissimae Augustae matri castrorum\n
EDCS-29700316.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, father of the fatherland, holder of tribunician power, consul, proconsul, and to Marcia Otacilia Severa, the most holy Augusta, mother of the camps.
EDCS-29700316.summary: This is a dedication honoring Emperor Philip the Arab and his wife Marcia Otacilia Severa, likely a milestone or dedicatory inscription, dated between 244 and 249 CE.
