EDCS-38100351.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Zmaragdus vixit nnos XXXX hic situs est cultorens sic templi Herculis id est Valeri Satyriani sub aedilicio Valeri Eutichetis et quaestura Laevi Iuliani faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-38100351.translation: To the departed spirits of Zmaragdus, who lived for 40 years. He rests here. The cultores of the temple of Hercules, namely Valerius Saturnianus, under the aedileship of Valerius Eutiches and the quaestorship of Laevius Julianus, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-38100351.summary: A funerary inscription for Zmaragdus, recording his death and burial, with the construction overseen by local magistrates and temple attendants.

EDCS-44200287.text_edition: \n mo veterano vixit annos LXXX Claudia Herete coniugi pientissimo et sibi faciendum et Iulius Martinus filius decurio sacerdotales sic municipii Drobetae curaverunt\n
EDCS-44200287.translation: To the departed spirits of [Mo] – a veteran, who lived for 80 years. Claudia Herete for her most devoted husband and for herself, and Julius Martinus, her son and decurion, priest of the municipality of Drobetae, took care for this to be made.
EDCS-44200287.summary: A tombstone for a veteran and his wife, indicating that both the veteran and his wife commissioned the monument, which was then supervised by their son who held a priestly office.

EDCS-44200290.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Fundanio Vestali Publius Fundanius SE bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-44200290.translation: To the departed spirits of Publius Fundanius Vestalis. Publius Fundanius SE placed this for his well-deserving [father].
EDCS-44200290.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Fundanius Vestalis by his son, Publius Fundanius SE.

EDCS-44200294.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus tribunicia potestate XVI consul III pontifex maximus et Imperator Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus tribunicia potestate II consul II divi Antonini Pii filii divi Hadriani nepotes divi Traiani Parthici pronepotes divi Nervae abnepotes restituerunt milia passuum VI\n
EDCS-44200294.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, with tribunician power for the 16th time, consul for the 3rd time, chief pontiff, and Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, sons of the deified Antoninus Pius, grandsons of the deified Hadrian, great-grandsons of the deified Trajan Parthicus, and great-great-grandsons of the deified Nerva, restored 6 miles of road.
EDCS-44200294.summary: An imperial rescript recording the restoration of 6 miles of road by Emperors Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus, dated to their respective consulships and tribunician powers.

EDCS-44200295.text_edition: \n Deae Dianae Amabilis Rufri Iusti clarissimi viri ancilla pro salute sua et suorum ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-44200295.translation: For the goddess Diana. Amabilis, slave of the most distinguished man Rufus Iustus, placed this votive offering for her safety and that of her family.
EDCS-44200295.summary: A votive offering dedicated to the goddess Diana by a slave woman for her own and her family's well-being.

EDCS-44200296.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Felix beneficiarius consularis legionis IIII Flaviae votum solvit libens merito aram posuit\n
EDCS-44200296.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Titus Flavius Felix, a beneficiarius of the consular legate of the IIII Flavia legion, willingly fulfilled his vow and set up this altar, as deserved.
EDCS-44200296.summary: An altar dedicated to Jupiter by a soldier serving as a beneficiarius in the IIII Flavia legion, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-44200297.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Iulius Argaeus Augustalis coloniae Scupinorum vixit annos LXX Vitales sic coniux eius vixit annos LV Maxima filia eorum vixit annos XXXVII Iulius Ingenuus miles legionis IIII Flaviae filius eorum parentibus piissimis posuit\n
EDCS-44200297.translation: To the departed spirits of Lucius Julius Argaeus, Augustalis of the colony of Scupinum, who lived 70 years; his wife Vitalis, who lived 55 years; and their daughter Maxima, who lived 37 years. Julius Ingenuus, a soldier of the IIII Flavia legion, placed this for his most devoted parents.
EDCS-44200297.summary: A family tombstone recording the lives of Lucius Julius Argaeus, his wife Vitalis, and their daughter Maxima, dedicated by their son who was a soldier.

EDCS-44200298.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Octavia Ingenua vixit annos XXV Octavius Leucianus pater et Iulius Argaeus coniugi carissimae posuerunt\n
EDCS-44200298.translation: To the departed spirits of Octavia Ingenua, who lived 25 years. Her father Octavius Leucianus and Iulius Argaeus placed this for their dearest wife.
EDCS-44200298.summary: A tombstone for Octavia Ingenua, dedicated by her father and husband.

EDCS-44800122.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Montano nomine Marci Luci Romani Marinius Augustalis coloniae Drubetae ex votum sic in possessione sua posuit\n
EDCS-44800122.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, in his aspect as Montanus, Marcus Lucius Romanius Augustalis of the colony of Drubeta, set this up in his own possession according to his vow.
EDCS-44800122.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Montanus by a local official of Drubeta.

EDCS-46700004.text_edition: \n Caius Luccius Capito miles legionis VII Claudiae optio Fortunae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-46700004.translation: Gaius Luccius Capito, a soldier of the seventh Claudian legion, a decurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Fortuna.
EDCS-46700004.summary: This is a votive inscription by a soldier from the Legio VII Claudia to the goddess Fortuna.

EDCS-46700005.text_edition: \n Deae Placidae Aelius Herculanus lapidarius ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus votum posuit\n
EDCS-46700005.translation: To the goddess Placidus, Aelius Herculanus, a stonecutter honored with decurional ornaments, set up his vow.
EDCS-46700005.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Placidus by an honored local official and artisan.

EDCS-46700097.text_edition: \n Caius Plotius Valens filio contra votum sibi et Ulpiae Dianillae coniugi et Plotiis Plotiae Plotinae Valentinae filiis vivos sic faciundum curavit\n
EDCS-46700097.translation: Gaius Plotius Valens arranged for this to be built for his son, against his own vow, for himself and his wife Ulpia Dianilla, and for his sons Plotius Plotus, Plotinus, and Valentinus, while he was still alive.
EDCS-46700097.summary: This inscription records a father's arrangement for a building project for his son and family, seemingly against his own previously made vow.

EDCS-50000146.text_edition: \n Marcus Aurelius Maximus Marcus Aurelius Decebalus Marcus Aurelius Candidus Marcus Aurelius Antonius Marcus Aurelius Mucathius Marcus Aurelius Bithus Titus Aelius Bithus Caius Iulius Victor Marcus Aurelius Bithus Titus V Marcus Aurelius Titus Iulius Marcus Aurelius Marcus Aurelius Titus Aelius Marcus V Titus Aurelius? Caius V\n
EDCS-50000146.translation: Marcus Aurelius Maximus, Marcus Aurelius Decebalus, Marcus Aurelius Candidus, Marcus Aurelius Antonius, Marcus Aurelius Mucathius, Marcus Aurelius Bithus, Titus Aelius Bithus, Gaius Julius Victor, Marcus Aurelius Bithus, Titus V, Marcus Aurelius, Titus Julius, Marcus Aurelius, Marcus Aurelius, Titus Aelius, Marcus V, Titus Aurelius(?), Gaius V.
EDCS-50000146.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly recording members of a family or a group, with several bearing the name Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-51500039.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Crescentilla vixit nnos XXVII Aurelius Valens veteranus ex beneficiario consularis coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-51500039.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Iulia Crescentilla lived 27 years. Aurelius Valens, a veteran and formerly a subordinate of the governor, set this up for his deserving wife.
EDCS-51500039.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Crescentilla, set up by her husband, a veteran.

EDCS-51500040.text_edition: \n Saeviae Basillae vixit nnis L hic sita est Marcus Antonius Fronto veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-51500040.translation: To Saevia Basilla, who lived 50 years, she rests here. Marcus Antonius Fronto, a veteran of the seventh Claudian legion, pious and faithful, set this up for his deserving wife.
EDCS-51500040.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saevia Basilla, set up by her husband, a veteran of the Legio VII Claudia.

EDCS-53701325.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavio Kapitoni liberto qui casu Viminacium Dasmini a latronibus atrocissimam mortem perpessus est Flavia Va mater filio S \n
EDCS-53701325.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Flavius Kapito, a freedman who met a most atrocious death at the hands of brigands near Viminacium Dasminus. Flavia Va, his mother, for her son.
EDCS-53701325.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedman named Flavius Kapito, who was killed by brigands, set up by his mother.

EDCS-54701316.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Baebio E<u=Y>tychi Augustali municipii Aeli Viminacii qui vixit annos LXV et Baebiae Marcellae eius Titus Baebius Abascantus Augustalis eiusdem municipii patronis \n
EDCS-54701316.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Titus Baebius Eutychus, an Augustalis of the municipality of Aelius Viminacium, who lived 65 years, and for Baebia Marcella, his wife. Titus Baebius Abascantus, an Augustalis of the same municipality, [set this up for his] patrons.
EDCS-54701316.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Baebius Eutychus and his wife Baebia Marcella, set up by their patron and fellow Augustalis.

EDCS-56900051.text_edition: \n Deo Mithrae Soli Aelius Cai libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-56900051.translation: To the god Mithras, the Sun, Aelius Cai willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-56900051.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras, the Sun, by Aelius Cai.

EDCS-57100067.text_edition: \n Severi Pertinacis et Marci Aureli <Aurel>(i) Antonini Pii Felicis Caius Iulius Hermo\n
EDCS-57100067.translation: To Severus Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix.
EDCS-57100067.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a dedication to the emperors Severus Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus. The name "Caius Iulius Hermo" appears at the end, possibly indicating the dedicator or a recipient.

EDCS-57100069.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Verina vixit annos XXX Grattius Severus ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus coniugi pientissimae\n
EDCS-57100069.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Verina lived for 30 years. Grattius Severus, honored with decurional ranks, [erected this] for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-57100069.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Verina, set up by her husband Grattius Severus, who held decurional office, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-57100071.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aelius Severus.
EDCS-57100071.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to Aelius Severus.

EDCS-57100072.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Mercuri qui vixit nnos XXV Lucius Licinius Polycarpus et Epictesis filio piissimo fecerunt\n
EDCS-57100072.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Licinius Polycarpus and Epictesis made [this] for their most devoted son, Mercurius, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-57100072.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mercurius, erected by his parents Lucius Licinius Polycarpus and Epictesis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-57100082.summary: This inscription is a brief dedication or memorial to Titus Cornelius Moschus.

EDCS-57200156.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Valerio Vitali vixit nnis LXV hic situs est et Luciae Valeriae filiae pientissimae vixit nnis XVIII hic sita est Valeria Hediste coniugi bene merenti et filiae dulcissimae se viva posuit\n
EDCS-57200156.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Valerius Vitalis lived 65 years and is buried here, and his most devoted daughter Lucia Valeria lived 18 years and is buried here. Valeria Hediste, while still alive, placed [this] for her well-deserving husband and her sweetest daughter.
EDCS-57200156.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Valeria Hediste for her deceased husband Lucius Valerius Vitalis and daughter Lucia Valeria, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-59500076.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Tiberi filio Saturnino IIvirali coloniae Ratiariae flamini primo municipii Aeliani item municipii Aeli Viminacii vixit annos LXX Marcus Castricius Iulianus quinquennalis coloniae Ratiariae avonculo sic bene de se merenti ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-59500076.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Castricius Iulianus, quinquennalis of the colony of Ratiaria, had this set up for his uncle, Gaius Julius Saturninus, son of Tiberius, former IIvir of the colony of Ratiaria and first flamen of the municipality Aelian, and also of the municipality Aelii Viminacii, who lived 70 years, who so well deserved of him.
EDCS-59500076.summary: This funerary inscription honors Gaius Julius Saturninus, a prominent local official, and was commissioned by his nephew Marcus Castricius Iulianus, a quinquennalis, likely from the late 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-59500077.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Petronius Marinus beneficiarius consularis legionis \n
EDCS-59500077.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. Petronius Marinus, a beneficiary of a consular governor of the legion...
EDCS-59500077.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Dolichenus by Petronius Marinus, a beneficiary of a consular governor of the V Macedonica legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-59500078.text_edition: \n MDKOSANQIDS beeneficiarius consularis legionis V Macedonicae l agens v voto posuit\n
EDCS-59500078.translation: M[...KOSANQIDS] beneficiary of a consular governor of the V Macedonica legion, acting [on behalf of ...] placed [this] by vow.
EDCS-59500078.summary: This is a votive inscription, possibly by a soldier named M[...KOSANQIDS] who was a beneficiary of a consular governor of the V Macedonica legion.

EDCS-59500079.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Luci filius Velina Dexter Aquileia veteranus legionis VII aedilis et decurio coloniae vixit annos XXXXV hic situs est Venustus et Arescusa liberti faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-59500079.translation: Lucius Valerius Dexter, son of Lucius, from the tribe Velina, a veteran of the VII legion, formerly aedile and decurion of the colony, lived 45 years and is buried here. His freedmen Venustus and Arescusa had [this] made.
EDCS-59500079.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Valerius Dexter, a veteran and former local official, erected by his freedmen, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-61800278.text_edition: \n Nomina mulierorum sic nuptarum Aurelia Bazis mater SP Aurelia Victorina M Aurelia Sissis Zenobi Aurelia Cunas Apolloni Aurelia Ursula Iuliani Aurelia Fortunata AEPE Aurelia Silvina MVC Antonia Prisca Alexandri Aure<li=IL>a Mamades Silvini Ulpia Graptilla Celeris Clodia Iusta Nati Aurelia Bonas CrisT \n
EDCS-61800278.translation: Names of women thus married: Aurelia Bazis, mother of SP Aurelia Victorina; M. Aurelia Sissis, daughter of Zenobius; Aurelia Cunas, daughter of Apollonius; Aurelia Ursula, daughter of Iulianus; Aurelia Fortunata, daughter of AEPE; Aurelia Silvina, daughter of MVC; Antonia Prisca, daughter of Alexandrus; Aurelia Mamades, daughter of Silvinus; Ulpia Graptilla, daughter of Celer; Clodia Iusta, daughter of Natus; Aurelia Bonas, daughter of Crispus.
EDCS-61800278.summary: This inscription lists the names of married women and their maternal or paternal relationships, likely as part of a social or genealogical record.

EDCS-64100126.translation: To the spirits of the departed Terentius Zeni, of the 7th Claudian legion, with 5 years of service...
EDCS-64100126.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Terentius Zeni, a soldier in the 7th Claudian legion, who served for five years.

EDCS-64100127.translation: To Aemilius Aurelius Secundus, veteran, formerly a decurion of the auxiliary alae of the Illyrians, who lived 70 years. Aemilius Aurelius Polytimus, Augustalis, [dedicated this].
EDCS-64100127.summary: This funerary monument honors Aemilius Aurelius Secundus, a veteran decurion of an Illyrian cavalry unit, who lived to be 70, and was likely dedicated by his son.

EDCS-64100128.text_edition: \n Numini Apollinis sacrum Publius Aelius Zoticus Augustalis coloniae Ratiariae ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus ab ordine splendidissimo \n
EDCS-64100128.translation: Sacred to the divine power of Apollo. Publius Aelius Zoticus, Augustalis of the colony of Ratiaria, honored with decurional ornaments by the most distinguished order.
EDCS-64100128.summary: This inscription dedicates a sacred space to Apollo, honoring Publius Aelius Zoticus, a local magistrate of Ratiaria who received honors.

EDCS-64100129.summary: This short inscription records that Fortunadus, also identified as Agathemeres, was the maker of the object.

EDCS-64100131.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Libero Patri Cornutianus ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-64100131.translation: Cornutianus placed this in fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest and Liber Pater.
EDCS-64100131.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Cornutianus dedicating an offering to Jupiter and Liber Pater.

EDCS-64100132.summary: This inscription appears to be a name, possibly of the person commemorated or the dedicator on a monument.

EDCS-64100134.text_edition: \n Deae Dardanicae Marcus Aurelius Valerianus beneficiarius consularis legionis IIII Flaviae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-64100134.translation: To the divine power of the Dardaniun Goddess. Marcus Aurelius Valerianus, a beneficiarius consularis of the IIII Flavia legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-64100134.summary: This votive inscription records Marcus Aurelius Valerianus, a staff officer in the IIII Flavia legion, fulfilling a vow to the Dardaniun Goddess.

EDCS-64100135.translation: To the Great Divine Power and Protector Serapis.
EDCS-64100135.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Serapis, invoking his power and role as protector.

EDCS-64100136.translation: Eufronius [dedicated this] as a vow to the Goddess Isis.
EDCS-64100136.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Eufronius fulfilling a vow to the Goddess Isis.

EDCS-64100137.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Vespasianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XVIIII pater patriae censor consul VIII peditibus et equitibus qui militant in cohortibus sex I Cretum I et II Bracaraugustanorum II Chalcidenorum VII Gallorum Ubiorum quae sunt in Moesia sub Sexto Vettuleno Ceriale qui quina et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerant item remigibus qui militant in classe quae est in provincia eadem sub eodem qui sena et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerant quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas \n
EDCS-64100137.translation: To the soldiers and cavalrymen serving in the six cohorts of Crete I, Bracaraugustani II, Chalcideni VII, Galli Ubii, which are in Moesia under Sextus Vettulenus Cerialis, who had completed twenty or more years of service, and likewise to the rowers serving in the fleet in the same province under the same commander, who had completed twenty or more years of service, whose names are listed below, he granted citizenship and the right of marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if any were unmarried, with those they would later marry, provided each man took only one wife.
EDCS-64100137.summary: This is an imperial rescript by Emperor Vespasian granting Roman citizenship to auxiliary soldiers and sailors who had completed long service in Moesia, dated to his ninth tribunician power and seventeenth acclamation as imperator.

EDCS-66500002.translation: To Mercury the Messenger, Ulpius Viator dedicated this.
EDCS-66500002.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mercury made by Ulpius Viator, likely a traveler or messenger himself, indicating a personal offering.

EDCS-67200316.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo -- Iulius In decurio coloniae\n
EDCS-67200316.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Julius, a decurion of the colony...
EDCS-67200316.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Julius, a decurion of a colony, the rest of the inscription being lost.

EDCS-67200317.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Titico vixit annos XL Aureliae! Severae sic et Aurelio! Aproniano sic bene merenti posuerunt Aureliae Bilae vixit annos LX et Ronelae vixit annos III\n
EDCS-67200317.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Titus lived 40 years. Aurelia Severa, and Aurelius Apronianus, well deserving, placed this for them. Aurelia Bila lived 60 years and Ronela lived 3 years.
EDCS-67200317.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aurelia Bila and possibly others for deceased family members, Aurelius Titus, Aurelia Severa, and Aurelius Apronianus, with their ages at death, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-67400429.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marci Iuli Marci liberti Papiria Eutychetis ornati ornamentis decurionalibus et Augustalis coloniae Ratiariae Iulii Sozon et Epaphroditus bene merenti\n
EDCS-67400429.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Marcus Julius, freedman of Marcus, Eutyches of the Papirian tribe, honored with decurional and Augustal ornaments of the colony of Ratiaria, and Julius Sozon and Epaphroditus, well deserving.
EDCS-67400429.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Julius Eutyches, a freedman honored with local magistracies in the colony of Ratiaria, dedicated by Julius Sozon and Epaphroditus.

EDCS-67400430.summary: This is a dedication made by Serapius in the city of Oescus, though the deity or purpose is not specified.

EDCS-67400480.text_edition: \n Deae Veneri Augustae sacrum Marcus Antonius Lupus IIvir item IIvir quinquennalis coloniae Scupinorum et Aelia Genthiane eius\n
EDCS-67400480.translation: Sacred to the Divine Venus Augusta. Marcus Antonius Lupus, duumvir, and also duumvir quinquennalis of the colony of Scupinorum, and his wife Aelia Genthiana dedicated this.
EDCS-67400480.summary: This is a dedication to Venus Augusta made by Marcus Antonius Lupus, a high-ranking magistrate (duumvir and quinquennalis) of the colony of Scupinorum, along with his wife Aelia Genthiana.

EDCS-67400481.text_edition: \n August-- sacrum Quintus Axenna Quinti filius Fabia Rufus decurio coloniae ob honorem aedilitatis locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-67400481.translation: Sacred to the Divine Emperor [Augustus?]. Quintus Axenna Rufus, son of Quintus, of the Fabia tribe, a decurion of the colony, for the honor of the aedileship, a site granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-67400481.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a site by Quintus Axenna Rufus, a decurion of a colony, for his aedileship, granted by the local council, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67500091.translation: To Mars of the Horsemen, for the safety of our lords the Emperors Severus and Antoninus. Atrius, a cavalryman of Cohort II Aureliae Dardanorum Antonininae.
EDCS-67500091.summary: This is a dedication to Mars Militaris by Atrius, a cavalryman in the auxiliary cohort II Aureliae Dardanorum Antonininae, for the well-being of Emperors Severus and Antoninus, likely dating to their reign (late 2nd to early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-68500336.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Flaminius Crispus, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, honored with decurional ornaments by the most illustrious order of the people of Ratiaria, lived 15 years. Here he lies. Lucius Flaminius Mar[tialis], his most unhappy father, had this made.
EDCS-68500336.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Flaminius Crispus, who died at age 15 and was honored with decurional ornaments, erected by his grieving father in the colony of Ratiaria.

EDCS-68500337.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Titus Iavennius Adiuctor and Alpiae Secundae, parents, and for the Iavennii...
EDCS-68500337.summary: A dedication to the deceased parents of Titus Iavennius Adiuctor and Alpiae Secundae, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-68500338.translation: As aedile, quaestor, duumvir, Titus Flavius Philodespotus, Augustalis of the colony of Ratiaria, to his well-deserving patrons.
EDCS-68500338.summary: A dedication by Titus Flavius Philodespotus, a local official in the colony of Ratiaria, to his patrons, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-69200449.text_edition: \n Hygiae Asclepio pro salutem sic domini <n=M>(ostri) Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti et Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti Pii Augusti et Iuliae Domnae Augustae matris castrorum Claudius Magnus medicus \n
EDCS-69200449.translation: To Hygieia and Asclepius, for the health of our lord, Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, and Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Augustus, and Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the camp; Claudius Magnus, physician.
EDCS-69200449.summary: A dedication by Claudius Magnus, a physician, to the gods Hygieia and Asclepius for the health of Emperor Septimius Severus, Emperor Caracalla, and Empress Julia Domna, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-69200450.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Severi nepoti Antonini Magni Pio sic filio Marco Aurelio Severo [Alexan]dro Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicie sic potestate VIIII consuli ter pater patriae milia passuum XXII\n
EDCS-69200450.translation: To Emperor Caesar, grandson of Severus, son of Antoninus Magnus Pius, Marcus Aurelius Severus [Alexander] Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 9th time, consul for the third time, father of the country, 22 miles.
EDCS-69200450.summary: An inscription recording the titles and achievements of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, including distances, likely from a milestone or a monument commemorating his reign around 220 CE.

EDCS-69200451.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Pate<r=P>no Lucius Pet<r=P>onius Timachus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae Severianae Alexandrianae ex optione votum posuit \n
EDCS-69200451.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus the Father, Lucius Petronius Timachus, a veteran of the 7th Claudian legion Severiana Alexandriana, from the rank of optio, dedicated his vow.
EDCS-69200451.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Lucius Petronius Timachus, a veteran of a specific Roman legion, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-70100063.translation: To Diana Augusta, for the health of Mummius Sisenna Rutilianus, imperial legate, and his children, by Titus Flavius Euangelus, Augustalis of the colony, through the care of his wife Pirata.
EDCS-70100063.summary: A dedication to Diana by Titus Flavius Euangelus, an Augustalis in a colony, for the health of the imperial legate Mummius Sisenna Rutilianus and his family, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-70200083.translation: She lived 40 years. Antonia Rufilla is buried here. [Her husband] made the dedication. [He asks that it be done] by his freedmen.
EDCS-70200083.summary: A tombstone inscription for Antonia Rufilla, recording her age and that her husband commissioned the monument, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-70200087.translation: To the Genius of the Emperors, the guild of metalworkers, under the prefecture of Gaius Helvius Titianus...
EDCS-70200087.summary: A dedication to the Genius of the Emperors by a guild of metalworkers, likely from the Roman province of Dacia during the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-70200089.translation: A vow to all the gods, Marcus Varius Politicus dedicated.
EDCS-70200089.summary: A dedication of a vow to all the gods by Marcus Varius Politicus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-70200090.translation: To Aesculapius of Ratiaria, a temple, a statue [was commissioned] by Hygia [---]. Antonius [---] for 300 sesterces.
EDCS-70200090.summary: A record of the construction of a temple and statue to Aesculapius in Ratiaria, mentioning a commissioner and a cost, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-70200091.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Domitia Ampelida lived for 60 years and is buried here. Marcus Domitius Maximus, alive, dedicated this for himself and his wife and children.
EDCS-70200091.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Domitia Ampelida and dedicated by Marcus Domitius Maximus for his family.

EDCS-70200092.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Aurelius Zmaragdus, Augustalis of the colony of Ulpia Ratiaria, honored with decurional ornaments, who lived 25 years, and for Septimia Saturnina, his wife, who lived [about] 11 years. For Septimia...
EDCS-70200092.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Aurelius Zmaragdus and his wife Septimia Saturnina, noting their age and status in the colony of Ratiaria.

EDCS-71100058.translation: To the divine... Titus Ster[t(inius)] Crispin[us], a soldier of the [Severian] Alexandrian legion, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-71100058.summary: An altar dedicated to a deity, likely Aptus, by Titus Stertinius Crispinus, a soldier of a Severian legion, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-71100062.translation: ...through Valerius Cosmus, decurion of the municipium Aelium Viminacium, and Aelius Iulianus, Aurelius Gaius, Aurelius Epicadus, Iulius Gaiianus, and Valerius Firminus, veterans of the aforementioned legion, as guardians.
EDCS-71100062.summary: A list of individuals, including a decurion and veterans, acting as guardians, likely associated with a legal or administrative matter concerning the municipium of Viminacium.

EDCS-71100067.translation: ...Valeria, daughter of..., by her testament, made this.
EDCS-71100067.summary: A funerary inscription recording that Valeria, daughter of [name missing], made this monument according to her own will.

EDCS-71100128.translation: Sacred to Diana. Titus Minic(ius) Opimianus, legate of the August Emperor, proconsul of the province of Upper Moesia.
EDCS-71100128.summary: A dedication to Diana by Titus Minic(ius) Opimianus, a high-ranking imperial legate in the province of Upper Moesia.

EDCS-71300205.translation: The Iuvensian potteries of the I Noricorum legion.
EDCS-71300205.summary: An inscription identifying the Iuvensian potteries belonging to the I Noricorum legion.

EDCS-71400601.summary: An inscription bearing the name and titles of the Roman Emperor Magnentius.

EDCS-72700152.translation: To Good Fortune, Fonteianus, by vow.
EDCS-72700152.summary: A votive dedication to the deity Bonus Eventus by Fonteianus.

EDCS-73200020.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Titus Claudius Licentianis(?), son of Titus Claudius L[--], in his second year, and for his father, who lived 30 years [...]. [---]antae, his wife, he placed this monument while alive; it shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-73200020.summary: A tombstone for Titus Claudius Licentianis(?) and his father, erected by the living and stating that the property will not pass to an heir.

EDCS-73900211.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to Hercules the Preserver, sacred for the health of our Emperors, Gaius Valerius Maximinus has paid his vow.
EDCS-73900211.summary: A votive dedication to Jupiter and Hercules made by Gaius Valerius Maximinus, likely in the 3rd century CE, for the safety of the emperors.

EDCS-74700584.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Coelius Firminus, son of Lucius, of the Romilia tribe, from Atesta, prefect of the 2nd cohort of Gauls, military tribune of the 4th Flavian Legion Felix, prefect of the 2nd Ala of Pannonians, decorated with honors for service in the Dacian War, twice chief magistrate of the colony of Atesta. He lived 30 years. Syrillus, freedman and heir, saw to its construction.
EDCS-74700584.summary: A tombstone for Coelius Firminus, a Roman official of equestrian rank, detailing his military and civic career and lifespan, erected by his freedman and heir.

EDCS-75000367.summary: A very brief inscription, likely a personal motto or dedication, referencing Constantine, possibly dating to the 4th century CE.

EDCS-75300186.summary: A simple funerary inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, Valerius Polo.

EDCS-76600334.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesare Augusti filio Augusto imperatore pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XXXV legio IIII Scythica legio V Macedonica\n
EDCS-76600334.translation: Under Emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 35th time, the 4th Skythian Legion, the 5th Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-76600334.summary: A historical notice mentioning Emperor Tiberius and two legions, likely part of a larger official document or inscription related to military activities or construction projects.

EDCS-79000099.translation: In the year 289, on the ninth day of Desius, during the festival celebrated for the goddess Pasikrata, who is established in the village of Junia Aurelia, I, holding the rights of three children, dedicated my slave named Kopryllos to the goddess Pasikrata, and I have handed over her price and possession; if anyone sets [this statue] alight, he will give five myriads to the most sacred treasury. Junia Aurelia with her foster-child and field Zoilus.
EDCS-79000099.summary: A dedication of a slave to the goddess Pasikrata by Junia Aurelia, likely from the 3rd century CE, also establishing a fine for desecration.

EDCS-81300042.translation: Fonteianus [paid] his vow to Bonus Eventus.
EDCS-81300042.summary: A votive dedication to the god Bonus Eventus (Good Fortune) made by Fonteianus.

EDCS-81400013.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-81400013.translation: Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan the Parthian, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 15th time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, to the cavalrymen who served in 11 alae [units of cavalry] called the 1st Ulpia of the Slingers, and the 1st Victorious Thracians, and the 1st of the Aravacian Spaniards, and the 1st of the Canninefates Roman citizens, and the 3rd Augusta Thracians which is in Upper Pannonia under Claudius Maximus, and similarly the 1st Flavia Augusta Britannica Milliaria and the 1st Veteran Thracian Archers and the 1st Praetorian Roman Citizens which are in Lower Pannonia under Nonius Macrinus, and the 1st Claudia Nova Mixed and the Flavian Gauls which is in Upper Moesia under Egilius Plarianus, and the 1st Flavian Gaetulians which is in Lower Moesia under Fuficius Cornutus, and the Felix Moesica which is in Upper Germania under Popilius Pedo, after completing service of 25 or more years and being discharged with an honorable dismissal by the procurator Varius Clemens, while they were on campaign in Mauretania Caesariensis, whose names are inscribed below: to those who did not have Roman citizenship, he granted it, and the right of marriage with the wives they then had, or with those they would subsequently marry, provided each only had one.
EDCS-81400013.summary: An extensive imperial rescript by Antoninus Pius, dated to 157 CE, granting Roman citizenship and marriage rights to auxiliary cavalrymen who had completed at least 25 years of service in various units across several Roman provinces.

EDCS-81400028.translation: [...] ordinary of the 4th Flavian Legion, to my sweetest, most longed-for, and incomparable wife.
EDCS-81400028.summary: A fragment of a tombstone or epitaph dedicated to a deceased wife by her husband, who was an officer in the 4th Flavian Legion.
